tasks.description: Audit current write-tool surface in src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py and existing tests to confirm every touch point before editing. Grep `post_simple_topic|post_sectioned_topic` across src/ and tests/ and re-verify (a) WRITE_TOOL_NAMES at topic_editor.py:43-51, (b) TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT body at :54-143, (c) TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS definitions at :219-295, (d) dispatch/validation branches at :1849-1850 (writeback), :1858-1890 (post_simple media + source-count guards), :1893-1905 (sectioned-requires-sections-or-blocks), :1915-1919 (rejected-action map), :2855 (publish), :3631-3647 (_summary_for_tool), (e) auto-shortlist suggested_actions builder at :2154-2160, (f) build_rejected_transition allowed-action set at :3870, and (g) the publisher path render_topic_publish_units at :4124-4246 (confirm a one-intro-block topic renders cleanly — intro branch at :4165-4216 skips title line for type=='intro'). Also enumerate related test files (tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py — esp. test_topic_editor_audit_action_vocabulary_excludes_invalid_rejected_actions at :1380-1437 and the post_simple media-rejection assertions at :305-337, tests/test_topic_editor_core.py, tests/test_topic_editor_media_understanding.py, tests/test_backfill_live_update_topics.py, tests/test_live_update_editor_*). Do not modify yet — produce a written enumeration in executor_notes.

In src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py, add the unified `post_topic` tool to TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS (insert immediately before `post_simple_topic` at ~:219 so it advertises first) and mark legacy aliases deprecated. The new `post_topic` JSON-schema entry must have: required=[`proposed_key`,`headline`,`source_message_ids`,`blocks`]; NO top-level `body` field; NO `sections` field; `blocks` declared with `minItems: 1`; block items reuse the existing block schema (type=`intro`|`section`, title?, text required, per-block source_message_ids, per-block media_refs in the existing canonical/shorthand shapes); optional fields `parent_topic_id`, `notes`, `override_collisions`. Description must frame the choice as editorial complexity (one beat vs many) — explicitly: `"One intro block = a single-beat story, even if it has media. Add section blocks only when the topic has distinct contributors, angles, or sub-stories that each independently merit a header."` Prefix the existing `post_simple_topic` (~:219-239) and `post_sectioned_topic` (~:241-295) tool descriptions with `"[DEPRECATED — use post_topic] "` and REMOVE the "use this for ANY media" steering from `post_sectioned_topic`'s description. Leave the legacy schemas otherwise unchanged so old model calls still validate. Extend `WRITE_TOOL_NAMES` (~:43-51) to include `"post_topic"`.

Extend dispatch in src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py with strict ordering: tool-name acceptance + schema validation (incl. minItems:1) → legacy structural guards (post_simple media check at :1858-1880 and source-count check at :1881-1890 — UNCHANGED so the existing `post_simple_cannot_attach_media_use_post_sectioned_topic` rejection still fires) → block normalization + default-media attachment → alias re-keying (post_simple_topic/post_sectioned_topic coerced into unified shape ONLY in a local variable for downstream validation/persistence; preserve original call["name"] for telemetry) → split blocks-required guards by tool name → collision check → persistence. Concrete edits: (1) Generalize the writeback at :1849-1850 to fire for `post_topic` too — `if call["name"] in ("post_sectioned_topic", "post_topic"): args["blocks"] = normalized_blocks`. (2) Extend `action_by_tool` at :1852-1856 with `"post_topic": "post_topic"` (legacy aliases keep their `post_simple`/`post_sectioned` action labels). (3) REPLACE the combined blocks-required guard at :1893-1905 with two SEPARATE per-tool branches — `post_topic` checks `not has_blocks` UNCONDITIONALLY (does NOT consult has_sections) and rejects with action=`rejected_post_topic`, reason=`post_topic_requires_blocks`; legacy `post_sectioned_topic` keeps its existing `not has_sections and not has_blocks` semantics and its existing `rejected_post_sectioned`/`post_sectioned_requires_sections_or_blocks` rejection. (4) Extend the rejected-action map at :1915-1919 with `"post_topic": "rejected_post_topic"`. (5) CRITICAL supporting infrastructure — extend `build_rejected_transition`'s allowed-action set at :3870 to include `"rejected_post_topic"` so post_topic rejections return tool_error instead of raising ValueError: `if action not in {"rejected_post_simple", "rejected_post_sectioned", "rejected_post_topic", "rejected_watch"}:`. Do NOT reorder steps — reordering breaks tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py:305-337 and/or admits the FLAG-004 stray-sections bypass.

In src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py at `_summary_for_tool` (~:3631-3647), add a new branch for `post_topic` that stores blocks-only — `{"blocks": args.get("blocks") or []}` — with NO top-level `body` and NO `sections`. The branch ordering: handle `post_topic` first, then preserve the existing `post_sectioned_topic` branch (legacy alias storage shape `{body, sections, blocks?}` from current lines 3632-3641 — do NOT drop `body`), then the existing `post_simple_topic` branch (`{body, media}` at line 3647 — unchanged), then `watch_topic` (unchanged). Confirm the call site passes the ORIGINAL `call["name"]` (not the alias-normalized name) so telemetry/storage shape selection stays tied to the original tool. The dispatch guard from T3 already guarantees args["blocks"] is non-empty for `post_topic` calls reaching this point, so no extra emptiness check is needed here.

In src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py at the auto-shortlist topic-summary builder (~:2154-2160), update `summary.suggested_actions` so it names `post_topic` instead of `post_sectioned_topic`. The resulting string should read e.g. `"post_topic, watch_topic/update sources, or discard_topic"`. Do NOT change the auto-shortlist threshold logic, the upstream active_topics payload assembly, or any other behavior in this area — this is a one-token string replacement plus any directly adjacent grammar. Closes FLAG-005 (the auto-shortlisted summary flows into `active_topics` and is a model-visible steering surface; it must not name the legacy tool).

Rewrite the `TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` constant in src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py (~:54-143). Required changes: (a) Rename role from "BNDC live-update topic editor" to "BNDC live-update writer" (or equivalent non-editor framing) at the role-label line. (b) Mention `post_topic` as the primary tool in the instructions; list `post_simple_topic` and `post_sectioned_topic` ONCE as "deprecated — accepted for backward compatibility; prefer `post_topic`". (c) REMOVE the rule "use sectioned for ANY media" at the current ~:101-105 and replace it with the editorial-minimalism rule: *"Use the minimum number of blocks that fits the story. A single creator dropping a single artifact = exactly ONE `intro` block with the media attached to it. Only add `section` blocks when the topic has genuinely distinct contributors, angles, or sub-stories that each independently merit their own header. If you find yourself splitting one creator's one video into 'The Video' / 'Audio' / 'Community Reaction' sections, you are wrong — collapse it to one block."* (d) ADD explicit brevity guidance: intro block body = 1-3 sentences, ~30-150 words; section block body = 1-2 sentences; no bullet lists; no filler restatement of the title. (e) ADD a concrete worked example: a one-creator one-video drop with two praise replies → exactly one intro block with the video as a `media_ref`, AND a labeled "don't do this" counter-example (same input split into three sections). (f) KEEP the per-block source-attribution rule, the per-block media-attachment rule, the canonical `media_ref` shape doc, and the no-global-Sources-footer rule. Also grep tests/ for any assertions on old prompt strings (e.g. "use sectioned for ANY media", "BNDC live-update topic editor") and update or delete those assertions as obsolete.

Read-only verification of the publisher path in src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py: trace `_publish_topic` (~:2861) → `render_topic_publish_units` (~:4124-4246). Confirm by reading the loop body at ~:4165-4216 that for a unified `post_topic` call producing one intro block, `normalize_topic_document` returns `[{type: "intro", title: None, text, source_message_ids, media_refs}]` and the function emits exactly one text unit (`header + "\n\n" + block_content`) followed by one media unit per `media_ref`, with NO orphan `**title**` section header (the intro branch must skip the title line for type=='intro'). Do NOT modify the publisher unless a test in T8 fails — record the trace in executor_notes either way.

Add/update tests under tests/. (A) NEW tests in tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py: (1) `test_post_topic_single_intro_block_with_media_accepted` — drives a `post_topic` call with one `intro` block containing one `media_ref`; asserts persisted topic has `summary.blocks` length 1, no `summary.body`, no `summary.sections`, and `render_topic_publish_units` produces `[text_unit, media_unit]` with no `**...**` section header markup. (2) `test_post_topic_multi_block_accepted` — one intro + two section blocks; asserts blocks length 3, per-block sources preserved. (3) `test_post_topic_rejects_empty_blocks_returns_tool_error` — empty `blocks` returns a `tool_error` transition with action `rejected_post_topic`, reason `post_topic_requires_blocks`; verifies `build_rejected_transition` accepts `rejected_post_topic` without raising. (4) `test_post_topic_rejects_empty_blocks_even_with_stray_sections` (FLAG-004 regression pin) — `blocks: []` PLUS non-empty stray `sections` array in args is rejected with action `rejected_post_topic`, reason `post_topic_requires_blocks` — the stray `sections` does NOT satisfy the contract. (5) `test_post_topic_collision_returns_tool_error` — colliding `post_topic` call returns a `tool_error` transition with action `rejected_post_topic` (not a raised exception). (6) `test_legacy_post_simple_topic_still_normalizes_via_alias_and_keeps_existing_storage` — legacy `post_simple_topic` call (body + source_message_ids, no media) is accepted and stored with the existing `{body, media}` shape. (7) `test_legacy_post_simple_topic_media_rejection_unchanged` — legacy media-bearing `post_simple_topic` still returns the structured rejection `post_simple_cannot_attach_media_use_post_sectioned_topic` (pinning :305-337 ordering). (8) `test_legacy_post_sectioned_topic_with_body_and_blocks_preserves_storage_shape` — legacy `post_sectioned_topic` call with `body` + `blocks` is accepted; stored summary keeps `body`, `sections`, and `blocks` exactly as today. (9) `test_auto_shortlist_suggested_actions_uses_post_topic_not_post_sectioned_topic` (FLAG-005 regression pin) — auto-shortlist builder for a shortlisted media topic emits `summary.suggested_actions` containing `"post_topic"` and NOT containing `"post_sectioned_topic"`. (B) UPDATE the existing action-coverage test `test_topic_editor_audit_action_vocabulary_excludes_invalid_rejected_actions` at tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py:1380-1437 to: add `"post_topic"` to BOTH `allowed_actions` and `configured_actions`; add `"rejected_post_topic"` to `allowed_actions`; add a `"post_topic"` entry to the configured-actions branch when `tool["name"] == "post_topic"`; and assert `build_rejected_transition(... action="rejected_post_topic" ...)` returns a payload with `action == "rejected_post_topic"` (mirroring the existing `rejected_watch` happy path). (C) NEW test in tests/test_topic_editor_core.py: `test_normalize_document_blocks_still_handles_legacy_body_plus_sections` — explicit regression pin that `normalize_document_blocks` is unchanged. (D) NEW end-to-end-style normalization test mimicking the NebSH "Last Party" scenario (one author, one video-bearing source message, one reply, two praise comments; model call emits one intro block) — assert (a) accepted, (b) `summary.blocks` length 1, (c) publisher emits exactly one text unit + one media unit, (d) no `**...**` section header markup. Drive against the deterministic dispatch entry point — do NOT call the real Anthropic API.

Run targeted then full test suites. (1) `pytest tests/test_topic_editor_core.py tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py -x -q`. (2) `pytest tests/test_live_update_editor_publishing.py tests/test_live_update_editor_lifecycle.py tests/test_backfill_live_update_topics.py tests/test_topic_editor_media_understanding.py -x -q`. (3) Full suite: `pytest -x -q`. If any test fails, read the error, fix the underlying code (NOT the test, unless the test is asserting on an obsolete prompt string the plan explicitly retired), and re-run until green. Additionally, write a short throwaway script that constructs the NebSH scenario (one author, one video drop, one reply, two praise comments) and feeds it through the topic-editor dispatch path to confirm the resulting topic has exactly one block + media attached and renders as `[text_unit, media_unit]` with no section header markup; run it; then delete the script. Do NOT create new persistent test files for the throwaway repro — the persistent regression coverage lives in T8.

watch_items: DO NOT REORDER the dispatch pipeline in T3. Strict ordering (tool-name acceptance + schema minItems → legacy structural guards → block normalization + default-media attachment → alias re-keying → split blocks-required guards → collision → persistence) is mandatory. Reordering breaks tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py:305-337 (FLAG-002) and/or admits the FLAG-004 stray-sections bypass.

FLAG-004 pin: `post_topic`'s blocks-required guard MUST check `not has_blocks` UNCONDITIONALLY and MUST NOT consult `has_sections`. A `post_topic` call with `blocks: []` + stray non-empty `sections` MUST be rejected as `rejected_post_topic` / `post_topic_requires_blocks`. Schema-level `minItems: 1` on blocks is the belt; the dispatch-level guard is the braces. T8 test #4 pins this.

FLAG-001/SD-005: extending `build_rejected_transition`'s allowed-action set at :3870 to include `rejected_post_topic` is NOT optional — without it, every post_topic rejection (collision, empty-blocks) raises ValueError instead of returning tool_error. T8 tests #3 and #5 plus the updated action-coverage audit test will fail loudly if this step is missed.

FLAG-003: the writeback at :1849-1850 MUST be generalized to fire for `post_topic` in addition to `post_sectioned_topic` so default-media-attached `normalized_blocks` reach `args` and `_summary_for_tool`. Missing this silently drops auto-attached media_refs from persisted post_topic summaries even though validation/transition payloads saw the normalized version.

FLAG-005: the auto-shortlist suggested_actions string at :2154-2160 is a model-visible steering surface (flows into `active_topics` payloads). The brief's out-of-scope list mentions auto-shortlist *threshold* logic only — the suggested_actions STRING is in scope for this plan (settled decision SD-004). Threshold logic is NOT touched.

SD-001 (alias storage preservation): legacy `post_simple_topic` keeps `{body, media}`; legacy `post_sectioned_topic` keeps `{body, sections, blocks?}` — do NOT drop `body` from legacy alias storage even when `blocks` is present. Only NEW `post_topic` calls produce blocks-only storage. This was a deliberate reversal of an earlier plan iteration to minimize downstream-reader compatibility risk.

SD-006 (telemetry continuity): `_summary_for_tool` is called with the ORIGINAL `call["name"]` (not a normalized name). Action labels: new `post_topic` calls emit `post_topic`; legacy aliases retain `post_simple`/`post_sectioned`. Do not pass a normalized name to `_summary_for_tool` or `action_by_tool`.

SD-008: `normalize_document_blocks` at :3957 is the immutable backward-compatibility contract for stored-topic READS and MUST NOT be modified. All schema changes flow through the write path; the read path is untouched. tests/test_topic_editor_core.py pins this.

Production-public cron: the live-update feed posts publicly on a cron. Prompt language changes must be conservative. Prefer additive rules over removing existing source/media safety rails. The system-prompt rewrite in T6 must KEEP the per-block source-attribution rule, per-block media-attachment rule, canonical media_ref shape doc, and no-global-Sources-footer rule.
High iteration count (5) — do NOT re-litigate settled flags or the eight settled decisions during execution. If a genuinely new concern surfaces mid-execution, escalate rather than churn.

Open plan questions: (a) deprecation logger.warning on alias use is NOT in scope unless explicitly requested — default to no warning emission; (b) downstream consumers (analytics/replays/archive jobs) filtering on action labels — preserve legacy alias action labels (`post_simple`/`post_sectioned`), new writes emit `post_topic`; surface any identified dashboard consumer as a follow-up rather than fixing it here.

Existing tests that assert on old prompt strings (e.g. "use sectioned for ANY media", "BNDC live-update topic editor") must be updated or removed as part of T6 — grep tests/ before changing the prompt constant.
Criterion 19 (manual smoke on dev cron tick) is `info`-priority and cannot be verified in CI — it requires a human to eyeball the next live-update cron tick post-merge.

sense_checks.question: Does the audit enumerate ALL of: WRITE_TOOL_NAMES (:43-51), TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT (:54-143), TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS (:219-295), writeback (:1849-1850), legacy media/source-count guards (:1858-1890), blocks-required guard (:1893-1905), rejected-action map (:1915-1919), publish (:2855), _summary_for_tool (:3631-3647), auto-shortlist suggested_actions (:2154-2160), build_rejected_transition (:3870), render_topic_publish_units (:4124-4246), and the relevant tests (test_topic_editor_runtime.py:1380-1437 and :305-337, test_topic_editor_core.py, test_live_update_editor_*, test_backfill_live_update_topics.py, test_topic_editor_media_understanding.py)?

Is the new `post_topic` tool entry listed BEFORE `post_simple_topic` in TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS, does its schema have `blocks: {minItems: 1}`, does it omit any top-level `body` field AND any `sections` field, are required fields exactly [`proposed_key`,`headline`,`source_message_ids`,`blocks`], are both legacy descriptions prefixed with `[DEPRECATED — use post_topic]`, has the "use this for ANY media" steering been removed from the `post_sectioned_topic` description, and does WRITE_TOOL_NAMES now include `"post_topic"`?

Are the dispatch edits ALL present and in the correct order: writeback at :1849-1850 generalized to fire for both `post_sectioned_topic` and `post_topic`; `action_by_tool` extended with `"post_topic": "post_topic"`; blocks-required guard SPLIT into TWO separate per-tool branches with `post_topic` checking `not has_blocks` UNCONDITIONALLY (not consulting `has_sections`) and rejecting with `rejected_post_topic` / `post_topic_requires_blocks`; rejected-action map extended with `"post_topic": "rejected_post_topic"`; AND `build_rejected_transition` at :3870 extended with `"rejected_post_topic"` in its allowed-action set? Do legacy structural guards (post_simple media + source-count at :1858-1890) still run BEFORE any alias re-keying or normalization?

Does the new `post_topic` branch in `_summary_for_tool` store `{"blocks": args.get("blocks") or []}` with NO `body` and NO `sections`? Are the legacy `post_sectioned_topic` `{body, sections, blocks?}` and `post_simple_topic` `{body, media}` storage shapes BOTH preserved verbatim from current behavior? Is `_summary_for_tool` still called with the ORIGINAL `call["name"]` rather than an alias-normalized name?

Does the auto-shortlist suggested_actions string at :2154-2160 now contain the token `post_topic` and NOT contain the token `post_sectioned_topic`? Were the auto-shortlist threshold logic, the upstream active_topics assembly, and any unrelated behavior left untouched?

Does the rewritten TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT (a) no longer contain "use sectioned for ANY media" or equivalent media→sectioned steering; (b) contain the editorial-minimalism rule that one creator + one artifact = one intro block; (c) contain explicit brevity guidance (intro 1-3 sentences ~30-150 words; sections 1-2 sentences; no bullet lists); (d) contain a worked example of a single-creator single-video topic as exactly one intro block AND a labeled "don't do this" counter-example; (e) use the role label "BNDC live-update writer" (or equivalent non-editor framing); (f) KEEP the per-block source-attribution rule, per-block media-attachment rule, canonical media_ref shape doc, and no-global-Sources-footer rule? Were obsolete tests asserting on the old prompt strings updated or removed?

Did the publisher trace confirm that a one-intro-block `post_topic` topic produces exactly one text unit (header + intro body) followed by one media unit per media_ref, with NO orphan `**title**` section header (the intro branch at ~:4165-4216 skips the title line for type=='intro')? If a deviation was found, was it noted for T8 to assert on?

Are ALL nine new runtime tests present (single-intro-block-with-media, multi-block, empty-blocks-rejected, empty-blocks-with-stray-sections-rejected, collision-rejected, legacy-simple-stores-existing-shape, legacy-simple-media-rejection-unchanged, legacy-sectioned-with-body-and-blocks-preserves-storage, auto-shortlist-suggested-actions-uses-post-topic)? Is the action-coverage audit test at :1380-1437 updated with `post_topic` and `rejected_post_topic` in `allowed_actions`, `post_topic` in `configured_actions`, and an explicit `build_rejected_transition(... action="rejected_post_topic" ...)` assertion? Are the `normalize_document_blocks` core regression test and the NebSH end-to-end-style normalization test both present?

Does `pytest tests/test_topic_editor_core.py tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py -x -q` pass? Does the targeted live-update + backfill + media-understanding suite pass? Does `pytest -x -q` pass across the full repo? Was the throwaway NebSH-repro script written, run (confirming one block + media attached, no section header markup), and DELETED?

user_actions.description: On the next dev-environment live-update cron tick after merge, manually eyeball the BNDC live-update feed: confirm that a single-author single-video topic posts as `title + one paragraph + media attached` (no separate "The Video" / "Audio" / "Community Reaction" sections), and that a genuinely multi-creator topic still posts as a multi-section document. This is success-criterion 19 (info priority) and cannot be verified in CI.
user_actions.rationale: The validation requires inspecting rendered Discord output on the live dev cron — the executor cannot drive a Discord UI render or wait for a cron tick.
user_actions.requires_human_only_reason: inspect_runtime_ui — requires a human to view rendered Discord messages produced by the production-shaped cron job in the dev environment.

meta_commentary: This plan touches a production-public cron path (BNDC hourly live-update feed). Iteration count is 5 — all 14 prior significant flags are verified-resolved and the 8 settled decisions (SD-001..SD-008) are non-negotiable; do not re-litigate them mid-execution. The critical structural invariants are: (1) dispatch ordering — legacy structural guards (post_simple media + source-count at :1858-1890) MUST run BEFORE any alias re-keying or normalization, otherwise tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py:305-337 breaks and FLAG-002 reopens; (2) `post_topic`'s blocks-required guard checks `not has_blocks` UNCONDITIONALLY (does NOT consult `has_sections`), with `minItems: 1` on the schema as a belt and the dispatch guard as braces — closes FLAG-004's stray-sections bypass; (3) `build_rejected_transition`'s allowed-action set at :3870 MUST be extended with `rejected_post_topic`, otherwise every post_topic rejection raises ValueError instead of returning tool_error (FLAG-001/SD-005); (4) the writeback at :1849-1850 MUST be generalized to fire for both `post_sectioned_topic` and `post_topic` so default-media-attached blocks reach persistence (FLAG-003); (5) the auto-shortlist suggested_actions string at :2154-2160 MUST be updated since it's a model-visible steering surface flowing into `active_topics` payloads (FLAG-005/SD-004); (6) legacy alias STORAGE shapes are preserved verbatim — only NEW `post_topic` calls produce blocks-only storage (SD-001); (7) `_summary_for_tool` receives the ORIGINAL `call["name"]` and selects shape per name (SD-006); (8) `normalize_document_blocks` at :3957 is untouched (SD-008). Tests at T8 are designed to fail loudly if any of these are missed — DO NOT weaken or skip them. Prompt rewrite (T6) language must be conservative: keep all source-attribution and media-attachment safety rails; only add brevity/minimalism guidance and remove the media→sectioned steering. Two open plan questions default to: no deprecation `logger.warning` on alias use (unless asked), and dashboards that filter on action labels are NOT updated in this plan (legacy alias action labels preserved; new `post_topic` action emerges as a new value — surface as a follow-up if a dashboard consumer is identified). Criterion 18 (file-size/separation-of-concerns judgment) and criterion 19 (manual cron-tick smoke) are inherently human-verification — covered by reviewer judgment and U1 respectively, not by automated tests.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1 — Audit the current write-tool surface and capture all touch points (including :2154-2160 auto-shortlist and :3870 build_rejected_transition)
Step 2 — Introduce unified `post_topic` tool with `blocks: minItems: 1`, no top-level `body`, no `sections`; deprecate legacy aliases

Step 3 — Extend dispatch with strict ordering: writeback generalization, action_by_tool extension, split blocks-required guards (FLAG-004), rejected-action map, build_rejected_transition allowed-action extension (FLAG-001)
Step 4 — Add `post_topic` branch to `_summary_for_tool` (blocks-only storage); preserve legacy alias storage shapes
Step 5 — Update auto-shortlist suggested_actions string at :2154-2160 from `post_sectioned_topic` to `post_topic` (FLAG-005)
Step 6 — Rewrite TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT (role label, remove media→sectioned rule, add minimalism + brevity guidance + worked examples)
Step 7 — Verify publisher renders a one-block topic cleanly (read-only trace of render_topic_publish_units)

Step 8 — Tests: new runtime tests (post_topic accept/reject/collision/stray-sections, legacy preservation, auto-shortlist), action-coverage audit update, core normalize_document_blocks regression, NebSH end-to-end normalization
Step 9 — Run targeted then broad pytest suites; throwaway NebSH repro script
Criterion 19 — manual smoke on next dev cron tick post-merge

validation.completeness_notes: All 9 plan steps map to exactly one task each (T1..T9) and the info-priority manual-verification criterion 19 is covered by U1. No orphan tasks. The plan's `subjective_judgment` criterion 18 (file size / separation of concerns) is covered by reviewer review of T2/T3/T4/T6 diffs — it is a verification-capability requirement on the reviewer, not a separable plan step.

baseline_test_note: Baseline-failure detection was not performed in this finalize phase (no test run executed). The executor should run `pytest tests/test_topic_editor_core.py tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py -x -q` BEFORE making any edits to record any pre-existing failures and distinguish them from regressions introduced by this change. Then run T9 after the implementation. All topic-editor / live-update tests are expected to be green on a clean main; any failure before edits should be triaged before proceeding.
